summary: Historical task graph retained for artifact traceability. The current executable plan is PLANS.md: a dedicated Relay desktop application for document search and OfficeCLI-backed Office file operations, with AionUi paths treated as historical references only.

phases.name: Current Architecture Baseline
phases.description: Tracks the product boundaries that already exist in source and must stay aligned with docs and verification artifacts.

tasks.title: OpenCode provider gateway is the live product path
tasks.description: The active product path is OpenWork/OpenCode using Relay as the first-run setup layer and OpenAI-compatible M365 Copilot provider gateway. The Tauri + Solid shell under apps/desktop is diagnostic support only.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-14 Desktop UI milestones)

tasks.title: Provider facade and diagnostic IPC contracts are canonical
tasks.description: The provider contract is the OpenAI-compatible HTTP facade in copilot_server.js. Rust source models and command signatures under apps/desktop/src-tauri remain canonical for diagnostic IPC only.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-12 Core / Client / IPC boundary refactor)

tasks.title: Execution ownership belongs to OpenCode/OpenWork
tasks.description: OpenCode/OpenWork owns sessions, tool execution, permissions, MCP routing, plugins, skills, workspace config, and transcript state. Relay-owned Rust execution is not a product fallback.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 provider gateway milestones)

phases.name: Repo Hardening Toward Claw-Code Operational Rigor
phases.description: Repository truth cleanup, headless doctor, deterministic parity/session harness, and CI alignment.

tasks.title: Clean up repository source of truth
tasks.description: Align workspace license metadata, remove dead workspace entries, set pnpm check as the canonical provider-safe frontend gate, and update live docs to the provider gateway product path.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-14 Repo hardening toward claw-code operational rigor)

tasks.title: Add headless relay-agent-doctor
tasks.description: Ship a dedicated doctor binary, shared diagnostics service, root pnpm doctor script, stable JSON report types, and integration coverage for ready/login-required/auth-failure/missing-workspace/missing-runtime-asset cases.
tasks.artifact: apps/desktop/src-tauri/tests/doctor_cli.rs

tasks.title: Retain deterministic provider and diagnostic coverage
tasks.description: Keep deterministic provider/diagnostic fixture coverage without linking or retaining the removed Relay-owned runtime/tools/compat harness crates.
tasks.artifact: cargo test --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --workspace --exclude relay-agent-desktop

tasks.title: Align CI with documented acceptance criteria
tasks.description: Replace Linux-only CI with Ubuntu and Windows coverage, run pnpm check plus diagnostic launch smokes, add docs truth guards, and keep release packaging separate from the main acceptance workflow.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-14 Repo hardening toward claw-code operational rigor)

phases.name: Provider-Only Hard Cut
phases.description: Remove remaining compatibility posture now that OpenCode/OpenWork is the product UX and execution owner. Relay keeps only provider gateway startup, M365 Copilot CDP transport, OpenCode/OpenWork config support, and diagnostics.

tasks.title: Make OpenCode provider gateway the only primary product path
tasks.description: Update live docs, scripts, task metadata, and diagnostics so first-use guidance starts with OpenCode/OpenWork plus start:opencode-provider-gateway. Desktop launch and smoke paths become diag:* checks only. Do not preserve legacy Relay desktop chat/session/tool execution compatibility.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Provider-only hard cut implementation)

phases.name: Diagnostic Shell Minimization
phases.description: Shrink the remaining Tauri/Solid shell so it is a provider gateway and diagnostics console, not a parallel chat/session execution product.

tasks.title: Replace chat-first desktop posture with provider diagnostics
tasks.description: Make the desktop first screen provider/diagnostic-oriented. Remove or isolate chat/session stores, approval UI, write undo UI, slash-command surfaces, MCP registry UI, and transcript rendering from the normal UI path. Legacy start_agent and continue_agent_session routing has since been deleted.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Diagnostic shell minimization implementation)

tasks.title: Retire legacy agent commands from public IPC
tasks.description: Remove Relay-owned chat/session execution commands from the public Tauri generate_handler list and frontend IPC bridge while leaving internal Rust diagnostic harnesses as deletion targets.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Legacy agent IPC retirement)

tasks.title: Delete legacy agent Tauri command module
tasks.description: Remove the now-unreachable Tauri command wrapper module for Relay-owned chat/session execution and guard against restoring it.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Agent command module retirement)

tasks.title: Retire dev-control legacy agent routes
tasks.description: Remove debug-only localhost routes and package aliases that could still start, continue, or approve Relay-owned desktop agent execution.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Dev-control agent route retirement)

tasks.title: Delete orphan desktop live harness scripts
tasks.description: Remove stale dev-control helper scripts and desktop-owned M365 live execution harnesses now that provider gateway live verification is the only supported M365 live path.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Orphan desktop live harness retirement)

tasks.title: Delete compat-harness crate
tasks.description: Remove the remaining standalone compatibility fixture crate and its vendored historical mock parity manifest now that provider gateway coverage is the supported verification surface.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Compat harness crate retirement)

tasks.title: Delete hard-cut agent wrapper
tasks.description: Remove the internal Relay-owned hard-cut agent controller and now-unused agent-loop config/semaphore plumbing so desktop diagnostics cannot start or continue Relay-owned execution turns.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Hard-cut agent wrapper retirement)

tasks.title: Retire legacy agent event and IPC types
tasks.description: Remove the remaining Relay-owned agent event payloads, session-history projection types, unsupported session-state entrypoints, frontend event listener bridge, and generated TypeScript contracts for the deleted desktop execution path.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-25 Legacy agent event/type retirement)

tasks.title: Retire legacy session registry and persistence
tasks.description: Delete the remaining Relay-owned in-memory session registry and Copilot session metadata persistence now that desktop execution commands, events, and IPC projection types are gone. Provider diagnostics should not expose Relay-owned chat session state.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 Legacy session registry and persistence retirement)

tasks.title: Retire legacy error taxonomy and stale agent-loop wording
tasks.description: Remove the unused Relay-owned execution error enum and stale agent-loop/session-registry naming after the legacy registry and persistence modules were deleted.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 Legacy error taxonomy retirement)

tasks.title: Retire orphan desktop chat UI and legacy browser mocks
tasks.description: Delete unused SolidJS chat/session UI modules and stale Playwright/Tauri mocks now that the desktop frontend is a provider diagnostics shell only.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 Orphan desktop chat UI retirement)

tasks.title: Harden OpenCode bash preflight policy
tasks.description: Add Relay-side token inspection for OpenCode bash tool requests so obvious destructive shell roots are denied before forwarding to the external runtime, including wrapper forms and mixed-case Windows verbs.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 OpenCode bash preflight hardening)

tasks.title: Remove stale office_search adapter fixture
tasks.description: Replace the last live Rust test fixture that mentioned the retired office_search tool and extend the hard-cut guard so copilot_adapter.rs cannot reintroduce it.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 office_search adapter fixture retirement)

tasks.title: Retire Relay workspace approval allowlist diagnostics
tasks.description: Delete the leftover Relay-owned workspace approval allowlist backend, IPC commands, generated/frontend wrappers, Settings UI, and mocks now that OpenCode/OpenWork owns tool permissions in provider mode.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 workspace approval allowlist diagnostics retirement)

tasks.title: Retire Relay workspace skills and slash-command diagnostics
tasks.description: Delete the leftover Relay-owned .relay/skills and .relay/commands discovery surfaces from backend, IPC, Settings, mocks, and desktop-core now that OpenCode/OpenWork owns skills and slash commands in provider mode.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 workspace skills and slash-command diagnostics retirement)

tasks.title: Retire Relay MCP registry diagnostics
tasks.description: Delete the leftover Relay-owned in-memory MCP registry commands and IPC types now that OpenCode/OpenWork owns MCP configuration and execution in provider mode.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 MCP registry diagnostics retirement)

tasks.title: Retire orphan LSP and workspace instruction diagnostics
tasks.description: Delete unused Relay-owned rust-analyzer probe and workspace instruction surface IPC after provider-mode LSP and workspace config ownership moved to OpenCode/OpenWork.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 LSP and workspace instruction diagnostics retirement)

tasks.title: Retire bundled OpenCode runtime sidecar
tasks.description: Remove Relay desktop startup and packaging of the bundled OpenCode runtime so OpenCode/OpenWork remains the external owner of UX, sessions, transcript, tools, and execution.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 bundled OpenCode runtime retirement)

phases.name: Live Provider Validation
phases.description: Tracks live signed-in M365 Copilot checks for the OpenAI-compatible OpenCode/OpenWork provider path.

tasks.title: Run live M365 OpenCode provider smoke
tasks.description: Run the OpenCode provider live smoke against signed-in M365 Copilot to verify both plain provider text and an OpenCode-owned read tool turn after the hard cut.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 live M365 OpenCode provider smoke)

phases.name: Release And Packaging Validation
phases.description: Tracks packaged desktop diagnostic build checks after the OpenCode/OpenWork hard cut.

tasks.title: Verify packaged desktop diagnostic build
tasks.description: Run the full Tauri package build after removing the bundled OpenCode runtime sidecar, then inspect the Linux package to confirm opencode-runtime is absent and retained diagnostic resources are present.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 packaged desktop diagnostic build)

tasks.title: Verify Windows installer release workflow smoke
tasks.description: Dispatch the GitHub-hosted Windows installer release workflow with an unsigned prerelease smoke tag, confirm the Windows job builds the NSIS installer, skips Trusted Signing as intended for unsigned prerelease mode, records the SHA256 digest, and publishes the installer asset to GitHub Releases.
tasks.artifact: docs/IMPLEMENTATION.md (2026-04-26 Windows installer workflow smoke)

phases.name: OpenWork/OpenCode First-Run Bootstrap
phases.description: Tracks managed first-run download, verification, launch handoff, and provider setup for OpenWork/OpenCode while preserving Relay outside UX and execution ownership.

tasks.title: Check OpenWork/OpenCode bootstrap feasibility
tasks.description: Confirm upstream OpenWork/OpenCode release artifacts, licenses, Windows x64 assets, digests, NPM package metadata, and the viable first-run bootstrap shape without reintroducing bundled Relay-owned execution.
tasks.artifact: docs/OPENWORK_OPENCODE_BOOTSTRAP_PLAN.md

tasks.title: Add pinned OpenWork/OpenCode bootstrap manifest
tasks.description: Add a source-controlled manifest for the selected Windows x64 OpenWork Desktop plus OpenCode CLI bootstrap artifacts, including exact versions, URLs, sizes, SHA256 digests, entrypoints, license notes, and schema validation.
tasks.artifact: bootstrap manifest schema and validation tests

tasks.title: Implement bootstrap downloader and verifier
tasks.description: Implement local download and verification for the pinned OpenWork/OpenCode bootstrap manifest. Store artifacts under app-local data, clean up partial downloads, verify size and SHA256 before marking artifacts installed, reuse existing verified artifacts, and expose structured diagnostics without installing or launching OpenWork/OpenCode yet.
tasks.artifact: bootstrap downloader/verifier module with tests

tasks.title: Add OpenCode CLI config smoke for bootstrapped artifacts
tasks.description: Exercise the pinned OpenCode CLI artifact path enough to prove the manifest/cache contract can support provider config setup before OpenWork installer work begins. Verify the OpenCode CLI entrypoint path and provider config installation against a temp workspace without invoking OpenCode-owned tool execution.
tasks.artifact: OpenCode CLI bootstrap/config smoke

tasks.title: Add OpenWork Desktop install/launch smoke
tasks.description: Define and implement a safe first OpenWork Desktop handoff smoke for the pinned Windows MSI path. The smoke must verify the OpenWork Desktop artifact and represent detection/launch status as diagnostics without silent install, Relay-owned UX, or OpenCode tool execution.
tasks.artifact: OpenWork Desktop bootstrap handoff smoke

tasks.title: Prepare live Windows OpenWork/OpenCode bootstrap E2E smoke
tasks.description: Prepared the original live Windows bootstrap preflight and runbook. The actual post-desktop-UX-removal acceptance run is now tracked by B12, which supersedes this earlier B06 live-run placeholder.
tasks.artifact: Windows bootstrap E2E preflight and runbook; superseded by B12 live acceptance

tasks.title: Add headless OpenWork/OpenCode bootstrap command
tasks.description: Add a production bootstrap entrypoint that downloads and verifies the pinned OpenCode CLI and OpenWork Desktop artifacts from the manifest, stores them under app-local data, and emits structured diagnostics without relying on the Relay desktop chat UX.
tasks.artifact: headless bootstrap command and smoke

tasks.title: Automate OpenCode extraction and provider config handoff
tasks.description: Extend the bootstrap flow so the verified OpenCode zip is extracted into the app-local cache, the entrypoint is probed, and Relay's OpenAI-compatible M365 Copilot provider config is installed into the target OpenCode/OpenWork workspace without executing OpenCode-owned tools.
tasks.artifact: automatic OpenCode extract/config bootstrap

tasks.title: Add explicit OpenWork installer approval handoff
tasks.description: Extend the bootstrap flow so the verified OpenWork Desktop MSI can be opened only after explicit operator approval, with installer path, version, SHA256, and provider handoff details recorded as diagnostics. Do not add silent install as the default path.
tasks.artifact: explicit OpenWork installer approval handoff

tasks.title: Start Relay provider gateway from bootstrap
tasks.description: Add a bootstrap-managed provider gateway startup path that creates or locates the Relay API key, starts the OpenAI-compatible provider endpoint, and returns the provider base URL, model, and API-key handoff expected by OpenCode/OpenWork.
tasks.artifact: bootstrap-managed provider gateway startup

tasks.title: Remove Relay desktop UX from the production path
tasks.description: Stop presenting the Solid/Tauri Relay desktop chat shell as a product UX. Keep only headless/bootstrap commands and diagnostic-only surfaces needed for provider gateway support, live M365 verification, and Windows packaging diagnostics.
tasks.artifact: Relay desktop UX removed or isolated as diagnostics-only

tasks.title: Run post-UX-removal Windows bootstrap E2E
tasks.description: Run a clean Windows E2E after Relay desktop UX is removed from the production path. Confirm first-run bootstrap downloads/verifies OpenCode/OpenWork, hands off the OpenWork installer with explicit approval, starts the Relay provider gateway, and completes provider text plus OpenCode-owned read tool turns through M365 Copilot.
tasks.artifact: post-UX-removal Windows bootstrap E2E readiness gate; 2026-05-12 Linux readiness-only and Windows x64 artifact download_verify reruns passed, headless/gateway/auto bootstrap smokes passed, M365 provider/read live smoke passed on one retry at /tmp/relay-live-m365-opencode-provider-YDGjaV after one structured-tool-call repair failure; Windows installer/browser handoff pending

tasks.title: Add no-thinking OpenWork/OpenCode auto bootstrap
tasks.description: Make the normal user entrypoint run the auto bootstrap path so users do not need to choose download, workspace config, provider gateway, or installer handoff flags. Keep non-Windows runs CI-safe and keep Relay out of OpenWork/OpenCode UX or tool execution.
tasks.artifact: one-command auto bootstrap entrypoint and smoke

tasks.title: Auto-configure OpenWork/OpenCode from installed Relay
tasks.description: Make the installed Relay desktop launch start the provider gateway, write the global OpenCode config, set relay-agent/m365-copilot as the default model when unset, and run the Windows OpenWork/OpenCode download/install handoff without requiring users to choose setup flags.
tasks.artifact: installed desktop auto provider/config bootstrap

tasks.title: Expose beginner OpenWork/OpenCode setup status and retry
tasks.description: Show a simple installed-app setup state so beginners can tell whether Relay is preparing, waiting for M365 sign-in, ready, or needs attention, and provide a one-click retry without exposing provider ports or token setup as required knowledge.
tasks.artifact: beginner setup status and retry UI

phases.name: Workspace Document Search SQLite/FTS Cutover Follow-up
phases.description: Post-MVP work split out from the broader SQLite/FTS cutover tuning bucket after diagnostics, readiness, support export, score, and metadata-boost telemetry reached the MVP completion line.

tasks.title: Run real-data SQLite/FTS search-quality evaluation
tasks.description: Evaluate representative local/shared folders to compare filename, derived-content, and SQLite/FTS evidence quality. Capture precision/recall-like examples, false positives, stale-evidence cases, score-weight recommendations, and privacy constraints without exporting original files or document text.
tasks.artifact: docs/WORKSPACE_DOCUMENT_SEARCH_SQLITE_FTS_EVALUATION.md

tasks.title: Define and implement primary-path SQLite/FTS cutover gate
tasks.description: Decide and implement when SQLite/FTS can move from advisory scoring to the primary search path. Include readiness thresholds, feature flags, rollback behavior, and Query Trace/support evidence that explains whether the active path is filename, derived-content, or SQLite/FTS primary.
tasks.artifact: feature-gated SQLite/FTS primary-path cutover with Query Trace, support export, display, and sync-journal rollback diagnostics

tasks.title: Measure and tune large-folder SQLite/FTS performance
tasks.description: Benchmark query latency, metadata/FTS write cost, DB size, WAL/SHM growth, checkpoint behavior, and scheduler/backpressure impact on large local and network-share-like folders. Record thresholds and remediation guidance without persisting or exporting document contents.
tasks.artifact: docs/WORKSPACE_DOCUMENT_SEARCH_SQLITE_FTS_PERFORMANCE.md

tasks.title: Promote index status to user-facing Workspace Search UX
tasks.description: Expose beginner-safe AionUi-visible index status, repair/retry affordances, and partial-result explanations. Keep raw DB paths, raw FTS rows, and document text out of normal UI and support exports while making degraded or rebuilding indexes understandable to users.
tasks.artifact: beginner-safe display index status, partial-result explanations, and repair/retry affordances

phases.name: Workspace Document Search Safety, Consistency, And UX Hardening
phases.description: Next implementation list after WDS01-WDS04. These tasks turn the completed SQLite/FTS diagnostics and user-facing index-state slice into a safer default path by adding cache protection, scoped cleanup, transactional index semantics, migration gates, quality regression coverage, ranking explainability, parser validation, and AionUi result-flow integration.

tasks.title: Implement cache quota, retention, and at-rest protection gate
tasks.description: Define and enforce separate quota/retention policy for metadata, ParsedDocument IR, derived FTS/table/preview indexes, result caches, pins, and search history. Gate content-bearing cache reads/writes on declared at-rest protection or an explicit local-cache policy, and expose metadata-only support diagnostics for cache size, quota pressure, protection state, and evictions.
tasks.artifact: content-bearing ParsedDocument and derived-content-index cache quota/protection gates with metadata-only quota, protection, and eviction diagnostics in executor, index report, and support export

tasks.title: Implement scoped root removal and derived-cache cleanup
tasks.description: Add a safe root-removal cleanup path that can delete only the selected root's metadata, parsed payloads, derived indexes, SQLite FTS rows, preview spans, and short-lived result caches after confirmation, while preserving unrelated roots, pins outside the root, search history policy, and job state.
tasks.artifact: confirmed remove-root cache and maintenance action that deletes only selected-root metadata, parsed payloads, derived indexes, SQLite FTS rows, and preview spans while preserving unrelated roots, jobs, user memory, pins, and search-history policy

tasks.title: Implement transactional content-index commit semantics
tasks.description: Make content indexing transaction-like: write parser output to staging, build required derived FTS/table/preview indexes from staged ParsedDocument records, atomically swap the active content index pointer only after all required derived artifacts complete, and keep the previous active index usable but marked stale on failures.
tasks.artifact: staged ParsedDocument and derived-content-index cache promotion, transactional SQLite derived-index writes, active content-index pointer swap, stale fallback, and metadata-only commit diagnostics

tasks.title: Implement schema migration and rebuild recovery gates
tasks.description: Add versioned migration handling for metadata, analyzer, parser pipeline, ParsedDocument IR, derived indexes, Evidence Pack, and result contracts. Record migration/rebuild health events, preserve durable workspace roots and user state, rebuild disposable indexes when possible, and open incompatible downgraded stores read-only instead of overwriting newer schema data.
tasks.artifact: metadata/ParsedDocument cache migration inspectors, SQLite read-only downgrade gate, schema migration gate health events, and metadata-only Query Trace/support export summaries

tasks.title: Add golden-query search quality regression gate
tasks.description: Create a privacy-safe golden-query harness for representative local/shared-folder search failures. Measure top-k expected coverage, folder-skew, forbidden false positives, unsupported-claim prevention, warning correctness, and latency budget without exporting original documents or unredacted extracted content.
tasks.artifact: privacy-safe synthetic golden-query gate, docs/WORKSPACE_DOCUMENT_SEARCH_GOLDEN_QUERIES.md, golden_query_regression quality warning, and metadata-only Query Trace golden-gate summary

tasks.title: Expose deterministic ranking and grouping score breakdown
tasks.description: Promote ranking diagnostics into an explicit score-breakdown contract for filename, keyword/FTS, table/cell, recency, pin/history, grouping, warning penalty, and hybrid merge contributions. Use the golden-query gate to prevent ranking changes that hide warnings or regress known expected files.
tasks.artifact: RelayDocumentSearchScoreBreakdown.v1 in product results, grouping score facts, Query Trace ranking totals, display labels, and metadata-only support export summaries

tasks.title: Add parser structure-profile validation gates
tasks.description: Validate Dedoc-compatible ParsedDocument output by selected structure profile. Ensure profile-specific parser output keeps tree nodes, tables, cells, annotations, metadata, warnings, and attachments separate, records lossy or unsupported fields as warnings, and rejects parser output that is only flattened text.
tasks.artifact: RelayParsedDocumentStructureProfile.v1 validation, flattened-text rejection, cache/derived-index gates, Evidence Pack structure-profile summary, executor diagnostics, and regression tests

tasks.title: Integrate AionUi result-flow continuation and stable selection
tasks.description: Wire the existing renderer-neutral result contract into the Relay-branded AionUi result flow so capped batches expose show-more/refine actions, selection remains stable across refresh and continuation, partial/index states remain visible, and Copilot prose stays secondary to structured result cards.
tasks.artifact: RelayDocumentSearchResultFlow.v1 display flow, AionUi result-flow envelope, MCP AionUi content, file-id stable selection keys, manifest/gateway metadata, and regression tests

phases.name: AionUi Release Acceptance And Windows Sign-off
phases.description: Post-WDS task list for moving the Relay-branded AionUi shell from completed document-search/result-flow contracts to a releasable Windows acceptance bundle. Linux-preparable gates are separated from clean-Windows sign-off, and B12 remains the existing Windows bootstrap handoff gate.

tasks.title: Create installed-app Windows acceptance matrix and evidence bundle
tasks.description: Define the installed Relay-branded AionUi acceptance matrix and evidence-bundle checklist. Map B12 bootstrap evidence, provider seeding, OfficeCLI/ripgrep, bundled Node/LiteParse, workspace document search, support export, and release workflow artifacts to concrete commands, expected outputs, and storage locations.
tasks.artifact: docs/AIONUI_WINDOWS_VALIDATION.md acceptance matrix and evidence-bundle checklist, plus docs/AIONUI_RELAY_MIGRATION.md installed-app acceptance boundary

tasks.title: Harden AionUi release workflow artifact manifest gate
tasks.description: Make release workflow verification assert the Relay-branded AionUi installer artifact, overlay version and upstream pin, bundled Node/LiteParse payload, unsigned or signed release mode, and generated manifest metadata before a prerelease can be treated as acceptable.
tasks.artifact: release-aionui-windows-installer.yml release artifact manifest gate, RelayAionUiReleaseArtifactManifest.v1 metadata in aionui-relay.json, release manifest upload, workflow/manifest/doc regression tests

tasks.title: Add pinned AionUi overlay application smoke
tasks.description: Run the Relay overlay against the pinned AionUi source or fixture and verify that provider seeding, beginner chrome hiding, /guid task launcher metadata, document-search result-flow contract, and bundled runtime payloads survive overlay application without manual edits.
tasks.artifact: scripts/apply-aionui-overlay.test.mjs pinned AionUi fixture application smoke covering provider seed, beginner chrome, result-flow files, MCP injection, and bundled ripgrep/relay-node/LiteParse payloads

tasks.title: Import clean-Windows B12 handoff evidence into AionUi acceptance
tasks.description: After B12 is complete on a clean Windows host, link its installer/browser handoff evidence into the AionUi acceptance bundle. Preserve exact artifact paths, provider endpoint/model, OpenCode/OpenWork versions, SHA256 values, and M365 text/read-tool transcript evidence.
tasks.artifact: Clean-Windows B12 installer/browser handoff evidence linked from the AionUi acceptance matrix

tasks.title: Run installed Relay-branded AionUi first-run provider smoke
tasks.description: On a clean Windows install, verify that the Relay-branded AionUi shell starts from the intended first-run flow, seeds the M365 Copilot provider/default model, surfaces sign-in or ready state clearly, discovers OfficeCLI/ripgrep/bundled Node/LiteParse, and does not route the primary user path through OpenCode Web.
tasks.artifact: Installed AionUi first-run provider and local-runtime smoke evidence

tasks.title: Run installed Workspace Document Search UX acceptance
tasks.description: Using the installed Relay-branded AionUi shell on Windows, run representative shared-folder searches and record result-card behavior, preview/open/copy/refine/show-more actions, partial/no-result/permission states, local draft and Copilot-polish boundaries, and support export output without source document contents.
tasks.artifact: Installed Workspace Document Search UX acceptance evidence and support-export sample

tasks.title: Publish AionUi release readiness decision
tasks.description: Consolidate the release workflow, overlay smoke, B12 handoff, installed first-run, and Workspace Document Search acceptance artifacts into a release readiness decision. Update docs with known limitations, unsupported Windows-only gaps, unsigned/signed build status, and the next operator action.
tasks.artifact: AionUi release readiness decision with evidence links and known limitations
